Second Petition For Order Granting Additional Time For Estate Administration
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe, Esq., counsel for and with Gemsy Kennedy, personal representative.

The Court expresses concern with respect to the personal representative's request to purchase assets of the estate. Ms. O'keeffe advises that the property in question is investment property held by an investment group which none of the other members expressed an interest in buying the decedent's interest. Ms. O'Keeffe further advises that certain members of the investment group had attempted to interest other third parties in purchasing the decedent's interest but were unsuccessful. Ms. O'Keeffe further notes that all beneficiaries of the estate have been notified of the personal representative's petition and no objections have been received or otherwise expressed to the personal representative.

The Court determines to grant the petition but notes that pursuant to Probate Code Sec. 9883, the Order need fully detail the terms and condition of the purchase. Upon inquiry from Ms. O'Keeffe, the Court will allow the attachment of the purchase contract to the Order to satisfy this requirement.

In light of the foregoing discussion and events, Ms. O'Keeffe requests a further grant of time to complete the administration of the estate. The Court will grant the same, extending the time to February 3, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody; Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (D204)

Petition For Order Allowing Sale Of Estate Assets To Personal Representative Under Probate Code 9883
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe, Esq., counsel for and with Gemsy Kennedy, personal representative.

The Court expresses concern with respect to the personal representative's request to purchase assets of the estate. Ms. O'keeffe advises that the property in question is investment property held by an investment group which none of the other members expressed an interest in buying the decedent's interest. Ms. O'Keeffe further advises that certain members of the investment group had attempted to interest other third parties in purchasing the decedent's interest but were unsuccessful. Ms. O'Keeffe further notes that all beneficiaries of the estate have been notified of the personal representative's petition and no objections have been received or otherwise expressed to the personal representative.

The Court determines to grant the petition but notes that pursuant to Probate Code Sec. 9883, the Order need fully detail the terms and condition of the purchase. Upon inquiry from Ms. O'Keeffe, the Court will allow the attachment of the purchase contract to the Order to satisfy this requirement.

In light of the foregoing discussion and events, Ms. O'Keeffe requests a further grant of time to complete the administration of the estate. The Court will grant the same, extending the time to February 3, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody; Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (D204)

Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Continued To Mar-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Preliminary Distribution And For Partial Compensation And Petition For Approval Of First Account
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for Gemsy Kennedy (Executor).
Douglas Grijalva appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. O'Keeffe stated that she has no objections, that Mr. Grijalva is asking for interest to be paid from the date the Decedent signed the marital settlement agreement, that all parties have been noticed and that nobody is objecting. Ms. O'Keeffe also stated that Mr. Grijalva has explained in detail, that she is okay with going from the date of the order and that her Client wants to do the right thing. The Court stated that its tentative is to grant the petition with accrued interest from the date of the order of August 11, 1999. Mr. Grijalva stated that Sandra signed the settlement agreement in January of 1999 and that he has been operating since that date. The Amended Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant the petition subject to review of the order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.
